book_summary: Numbers has chapters 1-6 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The opening wilderness block covers census, camp arrangement around the dwelling-place, Levite substitution, transport duties, camp purity, restitution, the suspected-adultery ordeal under explicit ethical critique, voluntary Nazirite consecration, and the Aaronic blessing. Safeguards reject militarization, census-based worth, tribal hierarchy, clerical superiority, unsafe sacred labor, misogyny, coercive ordeal reenactment, jealousy-as-proof, survivor-blaming, ascetic pressure, and shame around grief or bodies.

chapters.notes: Sinai census, named leaders, military-age roster, total, Levite exemption and sanctuary charge.
Camp standards and marching order with the tent of meeting and Levites at the center.
Aaronic line, Levite census, substitution for firstborn, clan service assignments, redemption silver.
Kohathite, Gershonite, and Merarite transport duties, covering procedures, service-age count, worker-safety and distributed burden.
Camp-boundary purity, restitution plus fifth, priestly portions, suspected-adultery ordeal preserved with anti-misogyny, anti-coercion, and anti-reenactment safeguards.
Voluntary Nazirite vow for man or woman, accidental interruption, completion offerings, and Aaronic blessing culminating in shalom.

translation_notes: Numbers/Bemidbar begins in the wilderness with communal organization around Divine presence.
Military-age census language is descriptive of camp organization and must not define human worth.
Those outside the census remain fully within the covenant community.
The camp is organized around the dwelling-place; Presence, not military power, occupies the center.
Numbers 5 restitution applies to man or woman and joins confession to principal return plus an added fifth.
Numbers 5 suspected-adultery ordeal is an ancient patriarchal, asymmetrical, coercive procedure and must never be reenacted or used to justify jealousy, accusation without evidence, or abuse.
Numbers 6 Nazirite vow is voluntary, time-bounded, and open to man or woman.
Accidental corpse contact interrupts the vow ritually but must not become moral blame for unexpected death.
The Aaronic blessing culminates in shalom—peace, wholeness, well-being, and restored relationship.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Numbers 5 camp-removal language requires anti-stigma and sanctuary-boundary audit.
Numbers 5 restitution should crosslink with Leviticus 5-6.
Numbers 5 suspected-adultery ordeal requires deep lexical, legal, gender, medical-history, and reception-history audit.
Numbers 5 outcome terminology involving belly/thigh requires textual and anatomical uncertainty notes.
Numbers 5:31 requires explicit anti-misogyny and anti-domestic-abuse safeguards.
Numbers 6 Nazir terminology requires lexical audit across Hebrew Bible and later Jewish reception.
Numbers 6 grape-product abstention, uncut hair, and corpse-contact boundaries require vow-practice audit.
Numbers 6 Aaronic blessing requires liturgical and reception-history crosslink.
next_actions: Continue Numbers with chapter 7: tribal leaders' offerings for dedication of the altar, repeated equal gifts, Levite transport resources, and voice from above the ark.
